structured_payload_candidate.title: Valve Authority Range for HVAC Coil Applications
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Valve authorities in the range of 30 to 50% are adequate for HVAC coil applications, with higher Δt coils requiring less valve authority. Authorities less than 30% should be avoided.

evidence_text:
1.4 2024 ASHRAE Handbook—HV AC Systems and Equipment  (SI)
• System integration with other building systems
• Other HV AC goals• Security and environmental health and safety criteria• Building envelope• Project timeline (design, equipment delivery, commissioning,
training, and construction)
• Basis of design• HV AC system analysis and selection matrix• System narratives• Budget costs (first cost, operating cost, and energy cost)• Final recommendation(s)
A brief outline of each of the final selections should be provided
along with overall budget and/or cost per square metre. In addition,
HV AC systems deemed inappropriate should be noted as havingbeen considered but not found applicable to meet the owner’s pri-mary HVAC goal.
The report should include an HV AC system selection matrix that
identifies the one or two suggested HV AC system selections (pri -
mary and secondary, when applicable), system constraints, and
other constraints and considerations.
judge_reason: Evidence does not support the claimed valve authority knowledge; evidence is generic text about HVAC system analysis report structure, not about valves.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low-Temperature Heating System Supply Water Temperature Range
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Low-temperature heating systems are generally designed with supply water temperatures from 80 to 115°C and temperature drops from 10 to 55 K.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Historical Hot-Water Heating System Design Parameters
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In the United States, hot-water heating systems were historically designed for a 93°C supply water temperature and an 11 K temperature drop.

structured_payload_candidate.description: jet remains in contact with surface, counteracts drop of cool airstream
structured_payload_candidate.title: Coanda Effect Description
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The Coanda effect causes a jet moving adjacent to a surface to remain in contact with the surface throughout the length of throw, counteracting the drop of a cool airstream.

evidence_text:
HVAC System Analysis and Selection 1.9
construction or renovation because most codes prohibit combusti-
ble material in a ceiling return air plenum. Ceiling plenums andraised floors can also be used for supply air displacement systems tominimize horizontal distribution, along with other features dis -
cussed in Chapter 4.
Some ceiling plenum applications with lay-in panels do not work
well where the stack effect of a high-rise building or high-rise ele -
vators creates a negative pressure. If the plenum leaks to the low-pressure area, tiles may lift and drop out when the outside door isopened and closed.
The air temperature in a return air plenum directly below a roof
deck is usually higher by 2 to 3 K during the air-conditioning season
than in a ducted return. This can be an advantage to the occupiedspace below because heat gain to the space is reduced.
judge_reason: Evidence discusses ceiling plenums and return air, not Coanda effect.

structured_payload_candidate.title: AHRI Standard 410 Coil Rating Parameters
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Coils are rated within parameters: entering air dry-bulb 18 to 38°C, wet-bulb 15 to 30°C, air face velocity 1 to 4 m/s, evaporator refrigerant saturation temperature –1 to 13°C, entering chilled-water temperature 1.5 to 18°C, water velocity 0.3 to 2.4 m/s.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Ethylene Glycol Solution Coil Rating Parameters
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For ethylene glycol solution: 0.3 to 1.8 m/s, –18 to 32°C entering dry-bulb, 15 to 27°C entering wet-bulb, 10 to 60% aqueous glycol concentration by mass.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dehumidifying Coil Sensible Heat Ratio Range
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies from about 0.6 to 1.0.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Standard Entering Air Conditions for Comfort Ratings
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The standard ratings at 26.7°C db and 19.5°C wb are representative of the entering air conditions encountered in many comfort operations.

evidence_text:
In completing this matrixassessment, the design engineer should identify the owner’s input tothe analysis. This input can also be applied as weighted multipliers,because not all criteria carry the same weighted value.
Many grading methods are available to complete an analytical
matrix analysis. Probably the simplest is to rate each item excellent,very good, good, fair, or poor. A numerical rating system such as 0to 10, with 10 equal to excellent and 0 equal to poor or not applica -
ble, can provide a quantitative result. The HV AC system with thehighest numerical value then becomes the recommended system toaccomplish the goal.
The system selection report should include a summary followed
by a more detailed account of the HV AC system analysis and systemselection. This summary should highlight key points and findingsthat led to the recommendation(s).
judge_reason: Evidence is about rating systems and report summaries, not entering air conditions.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Standard Air Density for Airflow Ratings
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Airflow ratings are based on standard air of 1.20 kg/m³ at 20°C and a barometric pressure of 101.325 kPa.
